texto: Unable to keep myself from temptation any longer, I reached up and ran my hand through his hair, which as much as he tried, never looked anything other than curly. I further melted into him when he started stroking my arm. Up and down, repeatedly, in sweet sensual delight. I tried to steady my breathing by taking deep, measured breaths. I refused to let him know just how much his touch was effectual and tantalizing.
explicacao: Citação de Rose Ann Bridges sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Unable to keep myself from temptation any longer, I reached up and ran my hand through his hair, …” articula amor, jodi, knot-a-liar em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como knot-a-liar e love-story.

frase_original: Unable to keep myself from temptation any longer, I reached up and ran my hand through his hair, which as much as he tried, never looked anything other than curly. I further melted into him when he started stroking my arm. Up and down, repeatedly, in sweet sensual delight. I tried to steady my breathing by taking deep, measured breaths. I refused to let him know just how much his touch was effectual and tantalizing.

texto: Looking at Sam was fine but it was the tasting I enjoyed most. Like the dew that watered fresh blossoms, Sam’s lips moved and left their mark against my heated skin. His scent of body wash and manliness– too inviting– left a delicious taste behind leaving me to desire the more I know I’ll never get.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Rose Ann Bridges: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Looking at Sam was fine but it was the tasting I enjoyed most.
